text.title: Codemod-First Refactor Agent Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Cross-cutting refactors touching > 20 call sites MUST be expressed as a codemod first; hand-edit is reserved for the residue.
rule.rationale: Codemod is reviewable as one transform; hand-edits multiply review surface.

rule.statement: Codemod MUST use an AST-aware tool (jscodeshift, ts-morph, ast-grep, comby, Roslyn refactor). Regex sed is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Regex misses string literals, comments, and macro contexts AST handles.

rule.statement: Codemod runs in batches (or one go); tests MUST run after each batch and fail the run on regression.
rule.rationale: Behavioural verification protects against semantic codemod errors.

rule.statement: Multi-agent setup splits roles: transform-author (opus) + reviewer (opus); transform-author cannot self-approve.
rule.rationale: Single agent shortcuts review; separation enforces second-pair-of-eyes.

rule.statement: PR body MUST include the codemod source + before/after diff samples + count of sites touched.
rule.rationale: Reviewers need the transform to reason about correctness, not just the diff.

rule.statement: If refactor < 5 files OR no AST tool exists for the language OR refactor needs semantic judgement per site, STOP and hand-edit.
rule.rationale: Codemod overhead beats benefit on small / un-codemod-able refactors.
